items.title: Merbok murder case: Post-mortems completed, identification pending
items.summary: SUNGAI PETANI: Post-mortem examinations have been completed on a married couple found dead in Kampung Belau, Merbok, but their bodies have yet to be released as formal identification is still ongoing. Kuala Muda district police chief ACP Hanyan Ramlan said the bodies remain at the Forensic Unit of Sultanah Bahiyah Hospital. "Although the post-mortems were completed yesterday, the bodies have not been handed over as DNA and fingerprint identification processes are still ongoing," he said.

items.title: 200-litre subsidised RON95 cap: Rakyat should not suffer from govt's shortcomings, says PN
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Perikatan Nasional (PN) has questioned the government over its planned cap on RON95 petrol subsidies, saying it is only a temporary fix that restricts consumption. Instead, the coalition has proposed a series of measures to cushion rising costs for the public. "Perikatan Nasional also questions why the government chose an approach that restricts rakyat's consumption.

items.title: Anwar arrives in Indonesia for talks with Prabowo on Middle East crisis and bilateral ties
items.summary: JAKARTA, March 27 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim arrived in Jakarta on Friday afternoon for a special visit to discuss escalating geopolitical developments in West Asia and strengthen Malaysia-Indonesia bilateral cooperation. The aircraft carrying Anwar landed at Halim Perdanakusuma Airport in East Jakarta at 3.45pm local time. The Prime Minister was received by Indonesian Foreign Minister Sugiono, Indonesia’s Ambassador to Malaysia Raden Datuk Mohammad Iman Hascarya and Malaysia’s Ambassador to Indonesia Datuk Syed Mohamad Hasrin Tengku Hussin.

items.title: Penang weighs WFH to ease impact of rising petrol and diesel prices
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: The Penang government is weighing the possibility of adopting a work-from-home (WFH) directive for civil servants following rising fuel costs linked to global conflict. Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow said the state would first study the federal government's directive before deciding how best to implement it locally. He said allowing some civil servants to work from home could help reduce physical commuting and ease the burden of higher transportation costs, particularly in light of escalating petrol and diesel prices driven by ongoing geopolitical tensions.

items.title: English text of Federal Constitution prevails until Agong says otherwise, Putrajaya tells courts
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 27 — The English version of the Federal Constitution is the nation’s authoritative legal document until the Yang di-Pertuan Agong declares otherwise, Putrajaya has asserted in its official defence against a lawsuit seeking to elevate the Bahasa Melayu version. It cited two landmark Federal Court rulings in the cases of Indira Gandhi and Loh Siew Hong as binding precedents that have already settled the matter. The lawsuit was filed last year by lawyer Haniff Khatri Abdulla, who is seeking a court declaration that the Bahasa Melayu version of the constitution should take precedence.

items.title: Karambunai state polls result stands as Election Court throws out Warisan challenge
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, March 27 — The Election Court on Friday struck out a Warisan candidate’s election petition seeking to nullify the Karambunai constituency election result in the 17th Sabah state election. High Court judge Wong Siong Tung, via Zoom, allowed all preliminary objections raised by the respondents to strike out the election petition filed against them. The petitioner, Ahmad Jais Otong, named the returning officer (RO) for the Karambunai state constituency election, the Election Commission (EC) of Malaysia and Perikatan Nasional’s PAS candidate, Datuk Aliakbar Gulasan, as the first, second and third respondents, respectively.

items.title: Driver dies after Isuzu D-Max plunges into drain in Sandakan crash
items.summary: The victim, identified as Chock Vui Heong, 50, was pronounced dead at the scene by medical personnel. Sandakan Fire and Rescue Station chief Jimmy Lagung said the department received an emergency call at 12.56pm before a team was dispatched to the location about seven kilometres away. He said the victim was extricated using special equipment, including hydraulic rescue tools, but was confirmed dead at the scene before the body was sent to hospital, while operations ended at 1.58pm after ensuring the area was safe.

items.title: KK court fixes May 25 mention in Shafie Apdal contempt proceedings linked to Zara Qairina inquest
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, March 27 — The Sessions Court has fixed May 25 for a mention in the committal proceedings involving former chief minister Datuk Seri Mohd Shafie Apdal to receive an update on a judicial review application seeking to quash the inquest into the death of Zara Qairina Mahathir. Shafie is alleged to have made remarks concerning the inquest that may amount to contempt of court, while the court had previously granted a stay of the committal proceedings pending updates on the judicial review. The court had earlier granted the Attorney General leave to commence contempt proceedings against Shafie and activist Che Muna Din, while a judicial review application filed by Pertubuhan Kebajikan Ekonomi Semangat Bersatu Malaysia challenging the inquest proceedings is opposed by the Attorney General’s Chambers, with respective counsel representing the parties.

items.title: MetMalaysia issues storm alert for parts of Sabah and Sarawak until 6pm
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 27 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a warning of thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds in Sabah and Sarawak until 6pm today. MetMalaysia said in a statement that Kapit (Belaga) and Limbang in Sarawak are affected. Meanwhile, the Interior (Sipitang and Beaufort) and West Coast (Papar and Penampang) in Sabah are also affected.

items.title: Iran engaging "intimate friends", including Malaysia, to ensure safe Hormuz passage
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Iran said it is actively engaging with its "intimate friends", including Malaysia, to ensure the safe passage of vessels through the Strait of Hormuz. It said countries, particularly in Southeast Asia, are already feeling the effects of increased oil prices and disruptions in supply chains arising from perceived threats to the Strait. "In light of these developments, Iran is actively engaging with its intimate friends to address the situation and ensure the safe passage of vessels.

items.title: Water levels drop across key Perak dams as dry spell intensifies
items.summary: Data from the National Water Services Commission (Span) war room portal and the Kerian Department of Irrigation and Drainage (DID) show falling levels at Air Kuning Dam in Taiping, Sultan Azlan Shah Dam in Kinta, and Bukit Merah Dam in Kerian. Bukit Merah Dam, which is under the supervision of the Perak DID, recorded a water level of 6.98m, or 34.77 per cent capacity, today— down from 7.01m (37.74 per cent) a day earlier. Meanwhile, Sultan Azlan Shah Dam recorded 243.80m, or 95.79 per cent capacity, while Air Kuning Dam stood at 42.20m, or 90 per cent.

items.title: 5E Resources set for IPO launch and ACE Market listing
items.summary: The IPO involves the issuance of 304.5 million new ordinary shares. This includes 77 million shares for the Malaysian public, 35 million shares for eligible directors, employees and contributors to the group, and 192.5 million shares via private placement to Bumiputera investors approved by the Ministry of Investment, Trade and Industry. In addition, 154 million existing shares will be offered for sale through private placement to selected investors.

items.title: Malaysian rights activist banned from entering Singapore
items.summary: SINGAPORE: A Malaysian activist who studied in Singapore has been denied entry after allegedly engaging in political activism there. In response to queries, the (Singapore) Ministry of Home Affairs (MHA) said on March 27 that Fadiah Nadwa Fikri had urged some youths to "adopt her brand of radical advocacy." The ministry added that she encouraged them to go beyond protests, mobilising students and communities in Singapore, and to undertake disruptive or violent actions to support specific causes.

items.title: Education Ministry upholds King's decree on student safety amid heatwave
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The Education Ministry upholds the decree of His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, to safeguard the safety and well-being of students, teachers and the school community, amid the current hot weather. Under current guidelines, schools are advised to postpone outdoor activities such as cross-country runs, camping, and drills if daily maximum temperatures exceed 35 °C for three consecutive days. The ministry also empowered school administrators to temporarily close schools if temperatures exceed 37 °C for three consecutive days, following a Level 2 heatwave warning.

items.title: Bad season for padi farmers as rice fields fail to yield
items.summary: KANGAR: A 14.4ha or 50 relongs of padi field in Sungai Batu Pahat here could not be harvested after facing a lack of water supply when the dry season began at the end of last year. Padi farmer Masri Abu Hassan, 43, said this season's rice crop was the worst he had ever experienced, as prolonged hot weather meant water could not be channelled into the padi fields. "I cultivated 10 relongs (2.88ha) of padi, but only four relongs could be harvested.

items.title: 25 years of absolute power: Evolution of Aston Martin Vanquish
items.summary: This month (March) marks a quarter-century since Aston Martin unleashed the V12 Vanquish, a nameplate that redefined the modern Super GT. The Pioneer: V12 Vanquish (2001-2007) When it broke cover at the 2001 Geneva Motor Show, the original Vanquish was a technological moonshot. The Carbon King: Second Generation (2012-2018) The second-generation Vanquish arrived in 2012, drawing heavy stylistic cues from the One-77 hypercar.

items.title: Store worker charged with trafficking over 3kg of meth in Tawau
items.summary: TAWAU: A 42-year-old store worker has been charged in the Magistrate's Court here with trafficking more than 3kg of drugs earlier this month. Hadriansyah Sabran acknowledged the charge when it was read before Magistrate Don Stiwin Malanjum, but no plea was recorded as the case falls under the High Court's jurisdiction. He is accused of trafficking 3,071.04g of methamphetamine at a vehicle storage facility in Lorong Yong Luk, Jalan Sin Onn, Tawau, at about 7.30pm on March 13.

items.title: Sibu man jailed four months for slashing girlfriend’s ex-husband with knife
items.summary: Romario also ordered for the sentence to run from the date of Maxwell’s conviction. However, upon arrival, the complainant got into an argument with Maxwell, who is said to be the boyfriend of the complainant’s ex-wife. During the altercation, Maxwell injured the complainant on the cheek using a sharp weapon.

items.title: Bukit Kwong dam at alert level amid dry spell
items.summary: KOTA BARU: The Bukit Kwong dam remains at alert level, with its storage capacity at 51.7 per cent as of March 25, amid the ongoing dry spell affecting parts of Kelantan. State Department of Irrigation and Drainage (DID) director Dr Siti Fairus Zakaria said the dam's water level remains within a controlled range but is under close monitoring due to a gradual decline. Siti Fairus said the Bukit Kwong dam was designed to serve multiple purposes, including flood mitigation, irrigation and agricultural supply, with water levels fluctuating depending on rainfall and inflow.

items.title: Nik Nazmi, Chegubard in talks to settle flood project defamation suit
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Former Natural Resources and Environmental Sustainability Minister Nik Nazmi Nik Ahmad and activist Badrul Hisham Shaharin are in negotiations to settle a defamation suit over statements linked to the river basin transformation flood mitigation projects. Nik Nazmi's lawyer, Yiow Ern Syenn, informed High Court judicial commissioner Gan Techiong that on Feb 25, they had sent a letter to Badrul Hisham outlining several settlement proposals. During the proceedings, Yiow also spoke on behalf of Badrul Hisham's lawyer, Razlan Hadri Zulkifli.

items.title: Fire rips through 11 homes in Batu Ferringhi, residents escape unhurt
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, March 27 — Residents of 11 houses in Kampung Din Manbo, Batu Ferringhi, here, went through anxious moments when their homes were destroyed in a fire this morning. Penang Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) assistant director (Operations) John Sagun Francis said 23 personnel with seven fire engines from the Teluk Bahang, Jalan Perak and Bayan Baru fire stations were dispatched to the scene after receiving an emergency call at 11.29am. “The fire involved 11 double-storey semi-permanent houses (Class B), with structural damage estimated at about 80 per cent.

items.title: Court to hear update on bid to quash Zara inquest on May 25
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: The Sessions Court has set May 25 for an update on a judicial review seeking to quash the inquest into the death of Zara Qairina Mahathir. Judge Amir Shah Amir Hassan set the date during today's Zoom mention for the committal proceedings against former Sabah Chief Minister Datuk Seri Mohd Shafie Apdal . "The court then asked parties to update it on the status of the judicial review in the High Court at the next mention on May 25, via Zoom.

items.title: [UPDATED] MACC urges formal reports over unverified claims of payments to politician
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has urged anyone with credible information on allegations involving payments to a politician to come forward and lodge an official report. The MACC was responding to allegations of a RM9.5 million payment said to have been made by businessman Victor Chin Boon Long to a group described as a "corporate mafia". Chin yesterday said the payment made to the so-called "corporate mafia" group was not a bribe, but a "service fee" paid under pressure to stop ongoing harassment.

items.title: Education Ministry reinforces safety measures in schools after King’s heat warning
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, March 27 — The Ministry of Education (MOE) upholds the decree of His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, to safeguard the safety and well-being of students, teachers and the school community, amid the current hot weather. Yesterday, in a post on the Facebook page of Sultan Ibrahim Sultan Iskandar, His Majesty emphasised the importance of safeguarding health by reducing outdoor activities exposed to hot and dry weather, and ensuring adequate water intake throughout the period. Sultan Ibrahim also said that the MOE should give serious attention to the matter, to ensure that the health of students is not adversely affected by the hot weather phenomenon.

items.title: Over 300kg of subsidised cooking oil seized in raid on Pasir Mas syndicate, says Kelantan Domestic Trade Ministry
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, March 27 — The Ministry of Domestic Trade and Cost of Living (KPDN) busted a syndicate for misappropriating over 300kg of subsidised cooking oil in Kampung Lubuk Tapah, Pasir Mas, yesterday. “…the suspects were caught moving boxes of subsidised cooking oil outside a house thought to be a transit point before the supplies were sold on the black market,” he said in a statement today. He said 12 boxes of subsidised cooking oil (204kg) were found outside the house, with another 119kg inside, totalling 323kg seized.

items.title: Election Court strikes out Warisan petition over Pitas state seat, orders RM30,000 in costs
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, March 27 — The Election Court has struck out a petition filed by Warisan candidate Datuk Harun Ismail challenging the outcome of the Pitas state seat, which was won by Gabungan Rakyat Sabah’s Datuk Seri Ruddy Awah. Kuching-based High Court judge Wong Siong Tung, who sat as the election court judge via a Zoom proceeding, delivered the decision after allowing preliminary objections raised by the respondents. Harun, 66, filed the election petition on December 23 last year, naming Ruddy, 57, as the first respondent, Pitas returning officer as the second respondent, and the Election Commission as the third respondent.

items.title: Fuel subsidy cap: Motorists back 200-litre limit as 'fair and manageable'
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Most motorists support the government's move to lower the monthly fuel subsidy limit to 200 litres, saying it is fair and unlikely to affect their daily expenses. The adjustment, announced by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, will reduce BUDI95 subsidy eligibility from 300 litres per month to 200 litres starting 1 April 2026. Retired supply chain manager Magesan, 59, said the new limit would not affect him as his monthly fuel usage is below 150 litres, but welcomed the policy as a push for more conscious fuel use.

items.title: Heavy congestion reported across key expressways
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Traffic volume on major expressways nationwide has surged as of midday today, with significant congestion and several bottlenecks reported in the northern, southern and Klang Valley sectors. According to the Malaysian Highway Authority (LLM) traffic management centre, as of 1.09pm, traffic is moving slowly along several stretches of the North-South Expressway (NSE) E1 in the north. At the Johor Causeway, southbound traffic remains heavy from the Sultan Iskandar Building (CIQ) towards Woodlands.

items.title: Hamzah calls for emergency Dewan Rakyat sitting over Middle East conflict impact
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The opposition has called for an emergency Dewan Rakyat sitting to discuss the domestic impact of the escalating conflict involving Israel, the United States, and Iran. Opposition leader Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin made the request in a letter dated March 26 to Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, which was also copied to the Dewan Rakyat Speaker. "Opposition members of parliament have received various questions and reactions from the public following the prime minister's announcement yesterday.

items.title: Johor Bomba turns to aerial firefighting as winds reignite sections of 150-hectare fire
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI, March 27 — An MI-17 helicopter of the Malaysian Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) Air Unit will begin water bombing operations to extinguish the fire on Jalan Bypass Punggai-Sebana Cove, here. JBPM said the extinguishing operation is still continuing in section G by ‘direct attack’ and ‘total flooding’ using ‘water tankers’ to control the spread of the fire. For the fire near PPR Pengerang, 55 per cent or 5.5 hectares of the 10 hectares affected area were extinguished using the ‘direct attack’ and ‘total flooding’ methods in sections E, F and C, with water from nearby fire hydrants.

items.title: E-hailing group urges review of Budi95 fuel quota, claims 800 litres insufficient
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: An e-hailing group has urged the government to review the Budi95 subsidy mechanism, claiming many drivers are forced to pay market prices for fuel after exhausting their quotas mid-month. Sahabat E-hailing Malaysia (SEM) said the current 800-litre monthly limit was insufficient for high-mileage drivers, some of whom consume up to 40 litres of petrol a day. SEM also criticised the different eligibility criteria and quotas set for e-hailing drivers, diesel-based vehicles, and airport taxis.

items.title: Merbok killings: Post-mortems done, couple’s bodies yet to be released pending forensic identification, say police
items.summary: SUNGAI PETANI, March 27 — Post-mortem examinations on the bodies of a married couple believed to have been murdered in an incident in Kampung Belau, Merbok, were completed yesterday. Kuala Muda district police chief ACP Hanyan Ramlan said, however, both bodies have yet to be released to their family and are currently at the Forensic Unit of Sultanah Bahiyah Hospital (HSB) in Alor Setar. It was reported yesterday that the bodies of three family members were discovered in a house in Kampung Belau, Merbok.

items.title: [UPDATED] Anwar to convene MBs, CMs over economic situation
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: The federal government will convene a meeting with all state leaders next week to address the current global economic pressures stemming from ongoing tensions in the Middle East. Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the move was part of measures to mitigate the global impact of the crisis, adding that he also plans to call leaders from all political parties. He said the meeting would be briefed by the National Economic Action Council, the National Security Council and relevant agencies to provide a clear picture of the current situation and the steps required moving forward.

items.title: In London's pubs, love gets a PowerPoint makeover
items.summary: LONDON: Fed up of swiping left or right on dating apps, young London singles are returning to a classic British way of meeting people – in the pub – with a modern twist: a PowerPoint presentation by a close friend pitching them as dating material. Organisers said all 150 tickets for the event, held at a north London pub, were sold in less than five minutes. Dating apps lose shine Dating apps are fading in popularity in the UK, with usage across the 10 biggest platforms falling 16% between 2023 and 2024, according to Global Dating Insights.

items.title: Malaysia eyes Petronas’ global investments for fuel supply buffer as US-Iran crisis worsens
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, March 27 — Malaysia is exploring ways to leverage Petronas’s diversified global investments in order to secure alternative supplies of fuel to stability supply in the country, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said today. “For now, supplies are still sufficient until May, but we are also seeking alternative options,” Anwar told reporters after Friday prayers here. Anwar reiterated that while current fuel supplies are assured until May, authorities are monitoring the volatile situation daily.

items.title: RON95 ban on foreign vehicles starts April 1: What you need to know
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Starting April 1, enforcement of the ban on the purchase of subsidised RON95 petrol by foreign-registered vehicles will take effect. The ban prohibits all foreign-registered vehicles from purchasing RON95 petrol, which is subsidised by the government for Malaysians. Previously, enforcement action was largely taken against petrol station operators found selling RON95 to foreign-registered vehicles.

items.title: Sabah, Sarawak retain diesel subsidies due to geography, costs and reliance on fuel, minister says
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, March 27 — The federal government’s decision to maintain diesel subsidies for Sabah and Sarawak reflects the unique geography and infrastructure challenges faced by residents in both states. “The distinct geography, logistical costs and development challenges in Sabah and Sarawak are carefully considered in every national policy decision, particularly given the relatively high diesel usage in both states. He said that residents in Sabah and Sarawak will continue to enjoy subsidised diesel at RM2.15 per litre under controlled usage, alongside the Budi Madani RON95 (BUDI95) quota priced at RM1.99 per litre with a monthly cap of 200 litres.

items.title: Maduro makes second appearance at New York courthouse
items.summary: NEW YORK: Venezuelan President Nicolas Maduro made his second appearance in a New York court on Thursday morning following his forcible seizure by United States (US) troops during a military raid in Caracas in early January, reported Xinhua. On Thursday, Maduro's lawyer continued pushing the judge to throw out the charges in court and argued that the US is violating Maduro's constitutional rights by blocking Venezuelan government funds from paying for his legal fees. On Jan 3, US military forces launched a large-scale strike against Venezuela and forcibly seized Maduro and his wife, Cilia Flores, before transporting them to New York.

items.title: School closure allowed if temperatures exceed 37 °C for 3 days
items.summary: IPOH: School administrators may temporarily close schools if temperatures exceed 37 degrees Celsius for three consecutive days. "State Education Departments and schools have sufficient experience in managing the current situation. "Outdoor school activities must also be suspended if temperatures range between 35 and 37 degrees Celsius for three consecutive days, including cross-country runs, camping, drills and other outdoor activities," he said.

items.title: [UPDATED] Anwar: Govt to explore all options to keep RON95 subsidy beyond May
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: The government will explore all available avenues to sustain the Budi Madani RON95 (Budi95) petroleum subsidy scheme beyond May, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said. He said Putrajaya would leverage all available resources, including Petroliam Nasional Bhd's (Petronas) global investments, to secure alternative petroleum sources from countries such as Brazil, Suriname and Canada. Anwar reassured that the government would maintain the subsidy until May.

items.title: Man charged with trafficking over 3kg of methamphetamine in Tawau
items.summary: TAWAU, March 27 — A man, who is also a store worker, was charged in the Magistrate’s Court here today with trafficking more than 3kg of drugs earlier this month. Hadriansyah Sabran, 42, nodded in understanding after the charge was read before Magistrate Don Stiwin Malanjum, but no plea was recorded as the case falls under the jurisdiction of the High Court. The accused is charged with trafficking 3,071.04 grams of methamphetamine at a vehicle storage facility premises in Lorong Yong Luk, Jalan Sin Onn, here on March 13 at about 7.30pm.

items.title: Anwar convenes meeting of all state leaders over US-Iran crisis
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, March 27 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim announced today that he will convene a meeting with all state leaders next week to ensure a coordinated national response to the ongoing conflict in West Asia. Speaking to reporters after Friday prayers here, Anwar said the meeting aims to brief the group on the measures being considered by the federal government, ensuring participation from all states, regardless of political affiliation. “God willing, next week I will convene all the menteri besar, the premier and the chief ministers to brief them so that all states, regardless of party affiliation, can participate together with the government, including the opposition,” he said.

items.title: Man on the run for four years charged with businessman’s murder in Mont Kiara
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 27 — An unemployed man who had been on the run for four years was brought before the Magistrate’s Court here today, on a charge of murdering a businessman in a residential area in Mont Kiara in 2022. VV Vadivelu, 39, along with three other individuals who had been charged earlier, is accused of murdering Goh Chok Chuan, 63, at a car park near the guardhouse of Amarin Kiara, Mont Kiara, here, at 11.40pm on July 11, 2022. The court set June 8 for mention.

items.title: National Integrated Immigration System to roll out in phases from March 31
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The National Integrated Immigration System (NIISe) will be rolled out in phases across all air, land, and sea checkpoints starting March 31. The Home Ministry said the implementation of the system is a strategic move to strengthen border security through a digitalised immigration framework that is more efficient, faster, and user-friendly. The rollout will begin with a pilot phase at selected locations, starting with Senai International Airport in Johor (air mode) on March 31, followed by the Bukit Bunga ICQS Complex (land mode) on May 31, and the Puteri Harbour International Ferry Terminal (sea mode) on June 30, the ministry said in a statement today.

items.title: Thirteen plead not guilty to rioting with weapons at Kuching eatery over alleged RM12,000 dispute
items.summary: KUCHING, March 27 — A total of 13 men claimed trial in the Magistrates’ Court here to a charge of rioting while using objects capable of being used as weapons at an eatery along Jalan Kuching City Mall. Deputy public prosecutor Muhammad Aidil Akmal Sharidan sought bail of RM5,000 with one local surety for each accused to ensure their attendance at all court proceedings. Abdurrahman granted bail of RM3,500, undeposited, with two local sureties for Lee’s clients, while the remaining three accused, who were unrepresented, were allowed bail of RM3,500 deposited with one local surety.

items.title: Employers urged to adopt flexible work strategy as energy costs rise, says HR Ministry
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, March 27 — The Ministry of Human Resources Malaysia (Kesuma) has called on employers to implement Flexible Work Arrangements (FWA) as a strategic measure to strengthen organisational resilience, reduce the impact of rising energy costs, and support sustainable economic growth. In a statement today, the ministry encouraged private sector employers to assess the suitability of adopting FWA based on operational needs, including through work-from-home (WFH) approaches, flexible working hours, job rotation or hybrid models. “Kesuma believes that implementing FWA offers comprehensive benefits to both employers and employees, particularly in the context of rising energy costs driven by geopolitical tensions and the reliance on daily commuting,” the statement said.

items.title: AMS Advanced Material eyes RM32.77mil from IPO ahead of ACE Market listing
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: AMS Advanced Material Bhd aims to raise RM32.77 million from its initial public offering (IPO) ahead of its listing on the ACE Market on April 23. The aluminium specialist company's IPO entails a public issue of 113 million new ordinary shares at 29 sen per share, representing 18.46 per cent of its enlarged issued share capital of 612.00 million shares. The IPO also comprises an offer for sale of 47 million existing ordinary shares through private placement to selected investors.

items.title: Schools in Malaysia may close if temperatures exceed 37°C for three days in a row, says deputy education minister
items.summary: IPOH, March 27 — School administrators may temporarily close schools if temperatures exceed 37°C for three consecutive days. “State Education Departments and schools have sufficient experience in managing the current situation. “Outdoor school activities must also be suspended if temperatures range between 35 and 37°C for three consecutive days, including cross-country runs, camping, drills and other outdoor activities,” he said.

items.title: Ringgit retreats to 4.00 versus US dollar amid Middle East ceasefire uncertainties
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The ringgit retreated to the 4.00 level against the US dollar at noon, as risk-off mode dominated market sentiment due to growing scepticism over a ceasefire in Iran after United States (US) President Donald Trump indicated that he would not commit to a deal with Tehran. "Following the announcement, the US Dollar Index (DXY) was seen climbing 0.28 per cent to 99.873 points, holding steady as safe-haven demand, while US benchmark equities indices fell more than one per cent," said Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd's chief economist Dr Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid. At 12.30pm, the ringgit stood at 4.0075/0105 versus the US dollar, marking its lowest position in two months, when it stood at 4.0045 on Jan 23.

items.title: Billions for nuclear? Charles Santiago says betting big on solar is better for Malaysia’s future
items.summary: PETALING JAYA, March 27 — Former Klang MP Charles Santiago today called on Malaysia to set a more ambitious renewable energy (RE) goal of at least 50 per cent by 2030, arguing that the current national target of 35 per cent is insufficient and leaves the nation vulnerable to global energy shocks. Speaking at a media conference on the impact of geopolitical conflict on climate change, Santiago, who is a former chairman of the National Water Services Commission (Span), said that while Malaysia has made progress, its continued reliance on fossil fuels is a risk amid rising tensions in the Middle East. “Given the ongoing geopolitical tensions and the need for greater energy independence, increasing domestic renewable energy capacity is essential for long-term resilience and sustainability,” he stated.

items.title: Father longs for daughter jailed in China, especially during Raya
items.summary: PASIR PUTEH: For almost two decades, former soldier Mohamad Lazim Jusoh has celebrated Hari Raya Aidilfitri without his eldest daughter, Umi Azlim, 43, who has been imprisoned in China. Hari Raya has never felt the same since May 2007, when Umi Azlim, then 24, was arrested in China, Lazim said. Umi Azlim studied biology at Universiti Malaysia Sabah.

items.title: Budi95 quota adjustment won't affect 90 per cent of Malaysians, says Saifuddin
items.summary: Pakatan Harapan (PH) secretary-general Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said daily life for 90 per cent of the population would continue as normal without additional costs. Saifuddin, who is also the home minister, said the temporary adjustment of the Budi95 eligibility limit from 300 litres to 200 litres a month, effective April 1, would remain in place until global conditions stabilised. "Today's world is facing increasingly challenging global pressures.

items.title: ‘Pow’ or ‘routine monitoring’? Wangsa Maju reps deny squeezing massage parlour for cash
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 27 — Two community representatives at the centre of a viral video allegedly showing them soliciting a bribe from a Wangsa Maju massage parlour have denied the accusation, insisting they were only conducting a “routine monitoring” visit in their official capacities. Vijeyndran Ravichadran, 39, deputy chairman of the Federal Territory Residents Representative Council (MPPWP) Sub Zone 2 for Wangsa Maju, and Paarthiydasan Ganesan, 33, chairman of Sub Zone 6 for Wangsa Maju, both filed police reports after an eight-minute CCTV clip of the incident was posted on the “Kelab Penyokong Perikatan Nasional” Facebook page. The video’s caption accused the pair of extorting (“pow”) the business.

items.title: Cut shower times in half, stop daily car washes, Span ex-chief urges Malaysians amid hot spell
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 27 — Malaysians are being urged to immediately slash their shower times in half and stop washing their cars daily, according to Charles Santiago, as a prolonged heatwave pushes critical water sources like the Sungai Muda to dangerously low levels. The former National Water Services Commission (SPAN) chairman said these everyday habits — often overlooked — are collectively draining millions of litres of water at a time when supply is tightening. Santiago pointed out that Malaysians use an average of 201 litres of water per person daily, significantly higher than the UN-recommended 165 litres.

items.title: Former Span chief pushes mandatory water recycling, rainwater harvesting as Malaysia’s dam levels dip
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 27 — Malaysia must shift from voluntary conservation to legally mandating large-scale solutions like industrial water recycling and rainwater harvesting to secure its water supply against increasingly frequent droughts, according to Charles Santiago. The former chairman of the National Water Services Commission (Span), warned that while public conservation efforts are important, they are not enough. Santiago also highlighted the potential of reclaimed water, treated wastewater that can be used for industrial processes and other non-potable activities, to further decrease dependence on the national water supply.

items.title: Lorry with modified tank leads police to 2,000-litre diesel stash in Kedah, suspect arrested
items.summary: JITRA, March 27 — Police have arrested a man and seized 2,250 litres of subsidised diesel believed to have been misappropriated in an operation, codenamed Op Tiris, at a petrol station in Bukit Kayu Hitam last Wednesday. Kubang Pasu police chief Mohd Radzi Abdul Rahim said the 32-year-old local man was detained at about 10am along with a lorry found in suspicious circumstances. “Inspection of the vehicle uncovered several items linked to diesel misappropriation activities, including subsidy cards belonging to other companies and a modified additional tank containing 250 litres of fuel,” he said at a press conference.

items.title: Police: Bystander slashed in KLCC melee after stepping in to stop alleged assault on tourist, 18 ‘photo touts’ held
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 27 — Police have arrested 18 men following a violent altercation near Kuala Lumpur City Centre (KLCC) that began with an alleged assault on a foreign tourist and escalated into a chaotic group attack, Dang Wangi police chief Sazalee Adam said in a statement today. Police said the victim sustained injuries to the head. Sazalee said all 18 suspects, aged between 17 and 45, were detained in an operation carried out in the vicinity of KLCC the following day.

items.title: Impersonation scams leave two Penang company directors nearly RM900,000 poorer, say police
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, March 27 — Two company directors in Penang have lost a combined total of nearly RM900,000 after falling victim to separate phone scam syndicates involving impersonation tactics. Penang police chief Datuk Azizee Ismail said the first case involved a 69-year-old woman who lost RM457,607 after being deceived by individuals posing as officers from the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission and the police. The victim, who was at her home in Simpang Ampat, received a call on March 1 from a man claiming her bank account was linked to a criminal case.

items.title: Ten years’ jail, five strokes for man who raped vulnerable PwD woman in Skudai
items.summary: Judge Madihah Zainol handed down the sentence after the accused, R. Sivanesan, pleaded guilty when the charge was read out by a court interpreter. In mitigation, Omar Kutty told the court that the accused had apologised and was remorseful for his actions. “The accused took advantage of the victim who has a physical disability, namely speech, hearing and learning disabilities.

items.title: Saifuddin Nasution: Govt keeps RON95 at RM1.99 to shield Malaysians from rising costs
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, March 27 — The Madani government remains committed to protecting the people from global economic pressures by maintaining the subsidised price of RON95 petrol at RM1.99 per litre under the BUDI Madani RON95 (BUDI95) programme, keeping Malaysia among countries with the lowest fuel prices globally. Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said the move comes as global crude oil prices have surged beyond US$100 per barrel, affecting many countries. “However, in Malaysia, the Madani government under the leadership of Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has taken a clear stance — the people must be protected.
